<div class="tooltip" style="color:green">UNITS<div class="right">This command sets the internal units for the code. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_u_n_i_t_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">LENGTH<div class="right">the units of lengths<i></i></div></div>=A <div class="tooltip">ENERGY<div class="right">the units of energy<i></i></div></div>=kcal/mol 
<span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat">The UNITS action with label <b></b> calculates something</span><div class="tooltip" style="color:green">WHOLEMOLECULES<div class="right">This action is used to rebuild molecules that can become split by the periodic boundary conditions. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_w_h_o_l_e_m_o_l_e_c_u_l_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ENTITY0<div class="right">the atoms that make up a molecule that you wish to align<i></i></div></div>=1-781 <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which molecules are reassembled<i></i></div></div>=1


<br/><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefmetad_short"><b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad","black")'>metad</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad">The READ action with label <b>metad</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">metad.bias</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>values from the column labelled metad.bias in the file named COLVAR_REWEIGHT</td></tr></table></span>:       <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">READ<div class="right">Read quantities from a colvar file. This action has <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefmetad");'>hidden defaults</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_r_e_a_d.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right">the name of the file from which to read these quantities<i></i></div></div>=COLVAR_REWEIGHT <div class="tooltip">IGNORE_TIME<div class="right"> ignore the time in the colvar file<i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">VALUES<div class="right">the values to read from the file<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad">metad.bias</b>
</span><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefmetad_long" style="display:none;"><b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad","black")'>metad</b>:       <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">READ<div class="right">Read quantities from a colvar file. This action uses the <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefmetad");'>defaults shown here</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_r_e_a_d.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right">the name of the file from which to read these quantities<i></i></div></div>=COLVAR_REWEIGHT <div class="tooltip">IGNORE_TIME<div class="right"> ignore the time in the colvar file<i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">VALUES<div class="right">the values to read from the file<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad">metad.bias</b>  <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which the file should be read<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">EVERY<div class="right"> only read every nth line of the colvar file<i></i></div></div>=1
</span><b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datweights1" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datweights1","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datweights1","black")'>weights1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datweights1">The REWEIGHT_METAD action with label <b>weights1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">weights1</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the weight to use for this frame to negate the effect the metadynamics bias</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">REWEIGHT_METAD<div class="right">Calculate the weights configurations should contribute to the histogram in a simulation in which a metadynamics bias acts upon the system. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_r_e_w_e_i_g_h_t__m_e_t_a_d.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div>  <div class="tooltip">TEMP<div class="right">the system temperature<i></i></div></div>=300 <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right"> the biases that must be taken into account when reweighting<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datmetad">metad.bias</b>

<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_mat" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_mat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_mat","red")'>zn69_filterbbb_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>zn69_filterbbb_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">zn69_filterbbb_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=2.9 D_MAX=5 NN=12 MM=24} 
<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_ones" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_ones","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_ones","blue")'>zn69_filterbbb_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>zn69_filterbbb_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">zn69_filterbbb_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=4
<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb","blue")'>zn69_filterbbb</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>zn69_filterbbb</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">zn69_filterbbb</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_mat">zn69_filterbbb_mat</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb_ones">zn69_filterbbb_ones</b>

This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd69");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_f_i_l_t_e_r__m_o_r_e.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">DATA<div class="right">the vector you wish to transform<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filterbbb">zn69_filterbbb</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that transform<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=3.2 D_MAX=5 NN=12 MM=24}  <div class="tooltip">LOWMEM<div class="right"> this flag does nothing and is present only to ensure back-compatibility<i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">LABEL<div class="right">a label for the action so that its output can be referenced in the input to other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd69" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd69","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd69_shortcut","blue")'>d69</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd69_shortcut">The MFILTER_MORE action with label <b>d69</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">d69</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">d69_highest</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the largest of the colvars</td></tr></table></span>  <div class="tooltip">HIGHEST<div class="right"> this flag allows you to recover the highest of these variables<i></i></div></div>

<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd67" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd67","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd67","blue")'>d67</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd67">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>d67</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">d67</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn67_filterbbb">zn67_filterbbb</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=3.2 D_MAX=5 NN=12 MM=24}

</span><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:

<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>

<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64","blue")'>d64</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>d64</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">d64</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn64_filterbbb">zn64_filterbbb</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=3.2 D_MAX=5 NN=12 MM=24} 
<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64_filtered" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64_filtered","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64_filtered","blue")'>d64_filtered</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64_filtered">The CUSTOM action with label <b>d64_filtered</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">d64_filtered</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn64_filterbbb">zn64_filterbbb</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datd64">d64</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<br/><b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datm" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datm","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datm","black")'>m</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datm">The COMBINE action with label <b>m</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">m</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn69_filter">zn69_filter.morethan</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn68_filter">zn68_filter.morethan</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn67_filter">zn67_filter.morethan</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn66_filter">zn66_filter.morethan</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn65_filter">zn65_filter.morethan</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn64_filter">zn64_filter.morethan</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datzn63_filter">zn63_filter.morethan</b>   <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO
<br/><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_short"><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefDRMSD_short"><div class="tooltip" style="color:green">DRMSD<div class="right">Calculate the distance RMSD with respect to a reference structure. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD");'>a shortcut</a> and it has <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefDRMSD");'>hidden defaults</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_d_r_m_s_d.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">REFERENCE<div class="right">a file in pdb format containing the reference structure and the atoms involved in the CV<i></i></div></div>=reference.pdb <div class="tooltip">LOWER_CUTOFF<div class="right">only pairs of atoms further than LOWER_CUTOFF are considered in the calculation<i></i></div></div>=0.1 <div class="tooltip">UPPER_CUTOFF<div class="right">only pairs of atoms closer than UPPER_CUTOFF are considered in the calculation<i></i></div></div>=8 <div class="tooltip">LABEL<div class="right">a label for the action so that its output can be referenced in the input to other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_shortcut","black")'>DRMSD</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_shortcut">The DRMSD action with label <b>DRMSD</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">DRMSD</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the DRMSD distance between the instantaneous structure and the reference structure</td></tr></table></span>
</span><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefDRMSD_long" style="display:none;"><div class="tooltip" style="color:green">DRMSD<div class="right">Calculate the distance RMSD with respect to a reference structure. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD");'>a shortcut</a> and uses the <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefDRMSD");'>defaults shown here</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_d_r_m_s_d.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">REFERENCE<div class="right">a file in pdb format containing the reference structure and the atoms involved in the CV<i></i></div></div>=reference.pdb <div class="tooltip">LOWER_CUTOFF<div class="right">only pairs of atoms further than LOWER_CUTOFF are considered in the calculation<i></i></div></div>=0.1 <div class="tooltip">UPPER_CUTOFF<div class="right">only pairs of atoms closer than UPPER_CUTOFF are considered in the calculation<i></i></div></div>=8 <div class="tooltip">LABEL<div class="right">a label for the action so that its output can be referenced in the input to other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_shortcut","black")'>DRMSD</b>  <div class="tooltip">TYPE<div class="right"> what kind of DRMSD would you like to calculate<i></i></div></div>=DRMSD
</span></span><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD")'># DRMSD REFERENCE=reference.pdb LOWER_CUTOFF=0.1 UPPER_CUTOFF=8 LABEL=DRMSD</span>

<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_diffs" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_diffs","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_diffs","blue")'>DRMSD_diffs</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_diffs">The CUSTOM action with label <b>DRMSD_diffs</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">DRMSD_diffs</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_d">DRMSD_d</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_ref">DRMSD_ref</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=(x-y)*(x-y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_u" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_u","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_u","black")'>DRMSD_u</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_u">The SUM action with label <b>DRMSD_u</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">DRMSD_u</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_diffs">DRMSD_diffs</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_2" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_2","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_2","black")'>DRMSD_2</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_2">The CUSTOM action with label <b>DRMSD_2</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">DRMSD_2</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>an arbitrary function</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_u">DRMSD_u</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=(x/304578 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD","data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD","black")'>DRMSD</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD">The CUSTOM action with label <b>DRMSD</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">DRMSD</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>an arbitrary function</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datDRMSD_2">DRMSD_2</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=sqrt(x <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6_short"><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefhB6_short"><div class="tooltip" style="color:green">HISTOGRAM<div class="right">Accumulate the average probability density along a few CVs from a trajectory. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6");'>a shortcut</a> and it has <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefhB6");'>hidden defaults</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_s_t_o_g_r_a_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> ...
   <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the quantity that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datCyss2">Cyss2</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datm">m</b>
   <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=0,0
   <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=30,10
   <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.4,0.4
   <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=300,300
   <div class="tooltip">LOGWEIGHTS<div class="right">the logarithm of the quantity to use as the weights when calculating averages<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datweights1">weights1</b>
   <div class="tooltip">LABEL<div class="right">a label for the action so that its output can be referenced in the input to other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6","data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6_shortcut","green")'>hB6</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6_shortcut">The HISTOGRAM action with label <b>hB6</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">hB6</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>the estimate of the histogram as a function of the argument that was obtained</td></tr></table></span>
   <div class="tooltip">NORMALIZATION<div class="right"> This controls how the data is normalized it can be set equal to true, false or ndata<i></i></div></div>=false
... HISTOGRAM
</span><span id="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefhB6_long" style="display:none;"><div class="tooltip" style="color:green">HISTOGRAM<div class="right">Accumulate the average probability density along a few CVs from a trajectory. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6");'>a shortcut</a> and uses the <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/plumed_fes_1_2.datdefhB6");'>defaults shown here</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_s_t_o_g_r_a_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> ...
   <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the quantity that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datCyss2">Cyss2</b>,<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datm">m</b>
   <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=0,0
   <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=30,10
   <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.4,0.4
   <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=300,300
   <div class="tooltip">LOGWEIGHTS<div class="right">the logarithm of the quantity to use as the weights when calculating averages<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.datweights1">weights1</b>
   <div class="tooltip">LABEL<div class="right">a label for the action so that its output can be referenced in the input to other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6" onclick='showPath("data/plumed_fes_1_2.dat","data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6","data/plumed_fes_1_2.dathB6_shortcut","green")'>hB6</b>
   <div class="tooltip">NORMALIZATION<div class="right"> This controls how the data is normalized it can be set equal to true, false or ndata<i></i></div></div>=false
 <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which to store data for averaging<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with whihc to clear the data that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=0
